name: ai
description: Tenant-scoped AI personas + tools + draft-then-confirm flow, built on top of the first-party `laravel/ai` SDK. Reference implementation lives at `.ref/AI` and is the source of truth for this blueprint. Every persona is a vendor `Laravel\Ai\Contracts\Agent` implementer using `Laravel\Ai\Promptable`. Every custom tool implements `Laravel\Ai\Contracts\Tool` via our `SensitiveTool` abstract base which adds compliance concerns (sensitivity classification, write-mode routing, role-gated authorization). Compliance concerns land as vendor `AgentMiddleware` classes returned from each persona's `middleware()` method. Streaming uses `->stream()->usingVercelDataProtocol()` (vendor-native). Conversation persistence uses `Conversational` + `RememberConversation` — vendor tables at `agent_conversations`. Failover cascades declared per persona via `#[Provider([Lab::Anthropic, Lab::OpenAI])]` and recorded to `ai_runs.provider_fallbacks_json`. Ships THREE owned entities: `AiRun` (single model invocation — model / provider / tokens_in / tokens_out / latency_ms / cost_minor / status per RunStatus enum; audit trail per conversation), `AiToolCall` (individual tool invocation inside an AiRun — tool_name + args_hash + result_summary + ms + error?), `AiDraft` (draft-then-confirm envelope for `WriteMode::Draft` tool invocations — payload + preview + expires_at + status per DraftStatus enum; separate `POST /ai/drafts/{id}/confirm` endpoint applies the draft after human review). Conversations are vendor-owned (`agent_conversations`, no owned entity). Optional `AiEmbedding` (pgvector RAG for SearchIndexerPersona). MCP support via `Mcp/AiToolAdapter` + `Mcp/ToolServer` — exposes tenant AI as an MCP endpoint for external agents (Claude Desktop, Cursor). Backend-first per D6 locked — browser NEVER holds provider keys.

vendor_packages.role: First-party vendor SDK — Agent + Promptable + Tool contracts, streaming pipeline, conversation persistence via Conversational + RememberConversation.
MCP server framework — Mcp/ToolServer exposes tenant AI tools over MCP.

attribute_driven.description: Five class-level attributes drive discovery + authorization. Discovered at boot by `ToolDiscoveryBootstrapper` (tools) and `PersonaResolver` (personas). Runtime authorization = pure hash-map lookup, never reflection.

attributes.AsAiTool: Marks a class as an AI tool discoverable by ToolDiscoveryBootstrapper. `#[AsAiTool(name: 'find_athlete', description: '…')]`. Name MUST be globally unique — bootstrapper throws DuplicateToolNameException on collision. Convention: snake_case verbs (find_*, get_*, create_*, draft_*, search_*, send_*, run_*).
attributes.Sensitivity: Per-persona ceiling — declares the strictest sensitivity class the persona may reach. Values from Sensitivity enum (Public/Pii/Financial/Medical/Safeguarding). A `Public` persona MUST NOT bundle a `Medical` tool (enforced at boot). `Safeguarding` is hard-denied regardless of consent.
attributes.RequiresRoles: Declares the spatie/permission role(s) required to invoke a persona. `#[RequiresRoles(['coach'])]`. Multiple roles = OR. Read by AuthorizePersonaRole middleware BEFORE reaching RunPersonaChatController. Canonical roles: owner / admin / branch_manager / coach / athlete / parent_guardian / front_desk / viewer / medical (sports only).
attributes.NeverAutoWrite: Persona-level marker (defence-in-depth). Every tool call — regardless of the tool's declared WriteMode — routes through the draft-then-confirm flow. Applied on MedicalAssistantPersona + AnomalyDetectorPersona.
attributes.AllowBackgroundWrite: Tool-level escape hatch. Marks a tool that MAY execute a direct write inside a background persona (bypasses draft flow). Currently only `upsert_embedding` on SearchIndexerPersona carries this. Interactive personas SHALL NOT bundle a tool carrying this attribute (verified at boot). Loses to `#[NeverAutoWrite]` on the persona.

personas.description: Eleven personas ship in the reference. Each maps to a spatie/permission role from the canonical Tenancy::defaultRoles() catalogue.

interactive.name: CoachAssistantPersona
interactive.focus: Athletes, teams, sessions, drills, scouting for own teams only

interactive.name: AthleteAssistantPersona
interactive.focus: Own schedule / progress / attendance / awards (v1.5 deferred)

interactive.name: FinanceAssistantPersona
interactive.focus: Revenue/expense reports, dunning drafts, membership status

interactive.name: MedicalAssistantPersona
interactive.focus: Injuries, clearances, RTP summaries — sports variants only

interactive.name: StaffAssistantPersona
interactive.focus: Daily branch operations, staff assignment, scheduling

interactive.name: ReceptionAssistantPersona
interactive.focus: Kiosk lookups, walk-in registration, day-pass sale, wristband issuance

background.name: AnomalyDetectorPersona
background.focus: Nightly attendance/injury pattern scan; files notifications only

background.name: SearchIndexerPersona
background.focus: Embeddings queue drain; uses upsert_embedding with #[AllowBackgroundWrite]

mcp_support.description: Tenant AI exposed as an MCP endpoint for external agents (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Zed). Enterprise-only feature per ai.mcp entitlement gate. Two files: Mcp/AiToolAdapter (adapts an AI Tool to an MCP tool contract) + Mcp/ToolServer (the MCP server). Every MCP tool invocation re-runs the SensitiveDataGate + persona-role check — the MCP transport doesn't bypass authorization.
mcp_support.authentication: Signed JWT bound to the tenant + a specific persona; expires per config.

tenant.description: Backend-first streaming — POST /api/ai/chat/{persona} with Vercel AI SDK data-stream protocol. Every route runs the four HTTP middleware (ai.feature / ai.rate / ai.reserve / ai.persona-role) BEFORE the vendor agent pipeline picks up the seven agent middleware (BindTenantContext / InjectTenantSystemPrompt / SensitiveDataGate / PiiAnonymizerPipe / RecordAiRun / EmitRunSummary / CheckCancellation).
